title: malus atrorubra
intro: Malus atrorubra, also known as the Chinese crabapple, is a species of apple tree native to China and Southeast Asia. The bark and roots of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Malus atrorubra include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Malus atrorubra can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, making it a valuable resource in traditional herbal medicine.
